seoTitle: One Mile vs One Tree — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between One Mile and One Tree. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogTitle: One Mile vs One Tree: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in One Mile and One Tree.
heading: One Mile vs One Tree — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between One Mile, New South Wales and One Tree, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: One Tree has a lower overall climate risk score (37/100) compared to One Mile (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is One Mile or One Tree safer from flooding?
faq.answer: One Mile has a flood risk score of 31/100, while One Tree scores 54/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: One Mile scores 22/100 for bushfire risk. One Tree scores 23/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in One Mile or One Tree?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. One Tree has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.58% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
